texto: The language itself, whether you speak it or not, whether you love it or hate it, is like some bewitchment or seduction from the past, drifting across the country down the centuries, subtly affecting the nations sensibilities even when its meaning is forgotten.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jan Morris: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “The language itself, whether you speak it or not, whether you love it or hate it, is like some be…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, language, wales pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de wales.

texto: Travel, which was once either a necessity or an adventure, has become very largely a commodity, and from all sides we are persuaded into thinking that it is a social requirement, too.
explicacao: Citação de Jan Morris sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Travel, which was once either a necessity or an adventure, has become very largely a commodity, a…” articula reflexao, adventure, thinking em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como viagem e thinking.

texto: It was an American who said that while a Frenchman's truth was akin to a straight line, a Welshman's truth was more in the nature of a curve, and it is a fact that Welsh affairs are entangled always in parabola, double-meaning and implication. This makes for a web-like interest....
